name: rainstorm

metadata.name: rainstorm
metadata.summary: Real & Artificial Intelligence Networks – Simple Tracker for Object Recognition Memory

documentation:
# RAINSTORM ### Real & Artificial Intelligence Networks – Simple Tracker for Object Recognition Memory ![RAINSTORM Logo](https://github.com/sdhers/RAINSTORM/blob/main/docs/images/logo.png)
**RAINSTORM** is a tool for scoring object recognition memory in mice 🐭. It allows users to automate the analysis of recognition memory performance through training of artificial neural networks. ### Features - Manual Frame by Frame behavioral labeling tool - Post-DeepLabCut data processing to avoid dissapearing bodyparts and glitching - Geometric labeling through distance and angle of aproach - Automatic labeling using a trained AI model able to detect temporal sequences of behaviour - Comparing labels in a visual and simple way ### Future steps - Multianimal labeling for social memories - Apply detection of moving objects for dinamic maze designs # Installation - Download Anaconda - Download RAINSTORM repository - In your terminal, navigate to where the repository was stored and run: conda env create -f conda_environments/rainstorm.yml # Pipeline - DeepLabCut analyzes video files and returns a .H5 file with the position of the mouse's bodyparts (along with two objects, in the case of object exploration). What we do next is up to us! ## Manage_H5 - It is important to filter from the position file the frames where the mouse is not in the video - Points that have a low likelihood assigned by DLC are filtered and data is smoothed - Also, it is convenient to scale the video from pixels to cm - Return: We obtain .csv files with the correct, scaled positions of the mice ![Example Manage_H5](docs/images/1-Manage_H5.png) ## Geometric_Labeling - One way of finding out when the mouse is exploring an object is to use a geometric criteria: - If the mouse is close to the object (distance < 2.5 cm) - If the mouse is oriented towards the object (angle < 45°) ![Example Geolabels](docs/images/2-Geometric_Labeling.png) ## Automatic_Labeling - Another way of finding out when the mouse is exploring is to train an artificial neural network with manually labeled data: ![Example Autolabels](docs/images/3a-Create_Models.png) Using TensorFlow, we were able to train models that are able to clasify a mouse's position into exploration ![Example Autolabels_2](docs/images/3a-Create_Models_simple.png) Among the models, we trained a more complex LSTM network that is aware of frame sequences, and performs better as exploration is time dependant. It trains based on our own manual labeling, so it acquires the criteria of the users. ## Compare_Labels - Once we have the manual, geometric and automatic labels, we can compare the performance of each on an example video: ![Example compare_1](docs/images/4-Compare_Labels_line.png) Using a polar graph, we can see for each position the angle of approach and distance in which the mice is exploring the objects - For a single video: ![Example compare_1](docs/images/4-Compare_Labels_polar.png) - Or for many videos together: ![Example compare_2](docs/images/4-Compare_Labels_polar_all.png) #### Since the automatic method learns to detect exploration unrestricted by the angle and distance to the object, it tends to be more accurate (Although, let's be honest... I chose to show you the best numbers I've ever gotten). ![Example compare_3](docs/images/4-Compare_Labels_result.png) ## Seize_Labels - We can use the best labels to evauate the performance of a mouse during the different sessions: ![Example seize_1](docs/images/5-Seize_Labels_example.png) - And finally, we can find differences in the exploration of objects for a group of trained mice (which was the obective all along): ![Example seize_2](docs/images/5-Seize_Labels_experiment.png) # In conclusion - This project, although already in use, is a work in progress that could significantly improve the way we analyze object exploration videos. - If you wish to contact us, please do so: simple.torm@gmail.com - © 2024. This project is openly licensed under the MIT License. #### Thanks for exploring us! ![Final_gif](docs/images/mouse_exploring.gif)

modules.name: rainstorm.utils
documentation: RAINSTORM - @author: Santiago D'hers
Functions for the notebook: 3a-Create_models.ipynb
%% Imports

functions.name: rainstorm.utils.broaden
documentation:
Build the frame window for LSTM training

Args:
    past (int, optional): How many frames into the past. Defaults to 3.
    future (int, optional): How many frames into the future. Defaults to 3.
    broad (float, optional): If you want to extend the reach of your window without increasing the length of the list. Defaults to 1.7.

Returns:
    list: List of frame index that will be used for training
%% Functions

functions.name: rainstorm.utils.recenter
documentation:
Recenters a DataFrame around a specified point.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to be recentered.
    point (str): Name of the point to be used as the center.
    bodyparts (list): List of bodyparts to be recentered.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Recentered DataFrame.

functions.name: rainstorm.utils.reshape
documentation:
Reshapes a DataFrame into a 3D NumPy array.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to reshape.
    past (int, optional): Number of past frames to include. Defaults to 3.
    future (int, optional): Number of future frames to include. Defaults to 3.
    broad (float, optional): Factor to broaden the range of frames. Defaults to 1.7.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: 3D NumPy array.

modules.name: rainstorm.evaluate_models
documentation: RAINSTORM - @author: Santiago D'hers
Functions for the notebook: 3b-Evaluate_models.ipynb
%% imports

functions.name: rainstorm.evaluate_models.create_chimera_and_loo_mean
documentation:
Creates a chimera DataFrame by randomly selecting columns for each row.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to create chimera from.
    seed (int, optional): Seed for reproducibility. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    tuple: A tuple containing the chimera DataFrame and the loo_mean DataFrame.
%% functions

functions.name: rainstorm.evaluate_models.smooth_columns
documentation:
Applies smoothing to a DataFrame column.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to apply smoothing to.
    columns (list, optional): List of columns to apply smoothing to. Defaults to [].
    kernel_size (int, optional): Size of the smoothing kernel. Defaults to 3.
    gauss_std (float, optional): Standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel. Defaults to 0.6.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Smoothed DataFrame.

modules.name: rainstorm.geometric_analysis
documentation: RAINSTORM - @author: Santiago D'hers
Functions for the notebook: 2-Geometric_analysis.ipynb
%% imports

functions.name: rainstorm.geometric_analysis.choose_example
documentation:
Picks an example file from a list of files.

Args:
    files (list): List of files to choose from.
    filter_word (str, optional): Word to filter files by. Defaults to 'TS'.

Returns:
    str: Name of the chosen file.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the files list is empty.
%% Functions

functions.name: rainstorm.geometric_analysis.plot_position
documentation:
Plot mouse exploration around multiple objects.

Args:
    file (str): Path to the .csv file containing the data.
    objects (list): List of objects to explore.
    maxDistance (float, optional): Maximum distance from the nose to the object. Defaults to 2.5.
    maxAngle (float, optional): Maximum angle between the head-nose and head-object vectors. Defaults to 45.

functions.name: rainstorm.geometric_analysis.plot_freezing
documentation:
Plots freezing events in a video.

Args:
    file (str): Path to the video file.
    fps (int, optional): Frames per second of the video. Defaults to 30.
    threshold (float, optional): Threshold for freezing events. Defaults to 0.01.

functions.name: rainstorm.geometric_analysis.create_movement_and_geolabels
documentation:
Analyzes the position data of a list of files and saves the results to a CSV file.

Args:
    files (list): List of files to analyze.
    objects (list): List of objects to analyze.
    maxDistance (float, optional): Maximum distance between the mouse and the object. Defaults to 2.5.
    maxAngle (float, optional): Maximum angle between the mouse and the object. Defaults to 45.
    fps (int, optional): Frames per second of the video. Defaults to 30.
    freezing_thr (float, optional): Threshold for freezing. Defaults to 0.01.
    darting_thr (float, optional): Threshold for darting. Defaults to 0.8.

modules.name: rainstorm.prepare_positions
documentation: RAINSTORM - @author: Santiago D'hers
Functions for the notebook: 1-Prepare_positions.ipynb
%% imports

functions.name: rainstorm.prepare_positions.choose_example
documentation:
Picks an example file from a list of files.

Args:
    files (list): List of files to choose from.
    filter_word (str, optional): Word to filter files by. Defaults to 'TS'.

Returns:
    str: Name of the chosen file.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the files list is empty.
%% functions

functions.name: rainstorm.prepare_positions.open_h5_file
documentation:
Opens an h5 file and returns the data as a pandas dataframe.

Args:
    path (str): Path to the h5 file.
    print_data (bool, optional): Whether to print the data. Defaults to False.
    num_sd (float, optional): Number of std_dev away from the mean. Defaults to 2.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: The dataframe contained in.

functions.name: rainstorm.prepare_positions.filter_and_smooth_df
documentation:
Filters and smooths a DataFrame of coordinates.

Args:
    data (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame of coordinates.
    bodyparts (list): List of bodyparts to filter.
    objects (list): List of objects to filter.
    med_filt_window (int, optional): Window size for median filtering. Defaults to 3.
    drop_below (float, optional): Minimum likelihood to keep a bodypart. Defaults to 0.1.
    num_sd (float, optional): Number of standard deviations to use as the threshold. Defaults to 2.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Filtered and smoothed DataFrame of coordinates.

functions.name: rainstorm.prepare_positions.find_scale_factor
documentation:
Plots the distance between ears and the mean and median distances.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame containing the coordinates of the points.
    measured_dist (float): Measured distance between points.
    measured_points (list): List of strings containing the names of the points.
    print_results (bool, optional): Whether to print the results. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    scale (float): The scale factor to convert the measured distance to the actual distance.

functions.name: rainstorm.prepare_positions.process_position_files
documentation:
Processes a list of HDF5 files and saves the smoothed data as a CSV file.

Args:
    files (list): List of HDF5 files to process.
    bodyparts (list): List of objects to process.
    objects (list): List of objects to process.
    measured_dist (float): Measured distance between points in cm.
    measured_points (list): List of reference points for the distance calculation.
    scale (bool): Whether to scale the data.
    fps (int): Frames per second of the video.
    drop_below (float): Drop values if the median likelihood is below this threshold.
    num_sd (float): Number of standard deviations to consider.

functions.name: rainstorm.prepare_positions.filter_and_move_files
documentation: Filters and moves files to a subfolder.

modules.name: rainstorm.first_steps
documentation: RAINSTORM - @author: Santiago D'hers
Functions for the notebook: 0-First_steps.ipynb
%% imports

functions.name: rainstorm.first_steps.copy_folder
documentation:
Makes a copy of a folder.

Parameters:
folder_path (str): Path to the original folder.
suffix (str): Suffix to add to the copied folder's name. Default is "_copy".

Returns:
str: Path to the copied folder.
%% functions

modules.name: rainstorm.labeler
documentation:
RAINSTORM - @author: Santiago D'hers
Use: RAINSTORM Labeler - Lets you label videos frame by frame with multiple behaviours

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.get_screen_width
documentation:
Workaround to get the width of the current screen in a multi-screen setup.

Returns:
    width (int): The width of the monitor screen in pixels.

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.resize_frame
documentation:
Resize frame for better visualization 

Args:
    img (np.uint8): Original image
    screen_width (int): The width of the fullscreen in pixels

Returns:
    new_img (np.uint8): Resized image

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.add_margin
documentation:
Add a margin to the frame, to write on it later

Args:
    img (np.uint8): Original image
    m (int): Width of the margin

Returns:
    new_img (np.uint8): Image with black margin

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.draw_text
documentation:
Generate text on an image

Args:
    img (np.uint8): Original image
    text (str): Text to be drawn
    font (optional): Text font. Defaults to cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_PLAIN.
    pos (tuple, optional): Text position. Defaults to (0, 0).
    font_scale (int, optional): Text size. Defaults to 1.
    font_thickness (int, optional): Text thickness. Defaults to 1.
    text_color (tuple, optional): Text color. Defaults to (0, 255, 0).
    text_color_bg (tuple, optional): Text background color. Defaults to (0, 0, 0).

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.process_frame
documentation:
Process a frame for labeling

Args:
    video_name (str): Video name
    frame (np.uint8): Frame to be labeled
    frame_number (int): Current frame number
    total_frames (int): Total number of frames
    behaviour_info (dict): Behaviour information
    screen_width (int): Screen width

Returns:
    tuple: Updates behaviour and move variables

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.find_checkpoint
documentation:
Find the checkpoint for the current frame

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): Labelled dataframe
    behaviours (list): List of behaviours to check for

Returns:
    int: Frame number of the last labeled frame

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.converter
documentation: Turns a variable into an integer if possible, otherwise returns the original value.

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.load_labels
documentation:
Load or initialize frame labels for each behaviour.

Args:
    csv_path (str): Path to the CSV file
    frame_list (list): List of frames
    behaviours (list): List of behaviours

Returns:
    tuple: Frame labels and current frame number

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.calculate_behaviour_sums
documentation:
Calculate the sum for each behaviour

Args:
    frame_labels (dict): Labels for each frame
    behaviours (list): List of behaviours

Returns:
    list: Sum for each behaviour

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.build_behaviour_info
documentation:
Build the behaviour_info dictionary with key mappings, sums, and current behaviour status.

Args:
    behaviours (list): List of behaviours
    keys (list): List of keys
    behaviour_sums (list): Sum for each behaviour
    current_behaviour (list): Current behaviour status for each behaviour

Returns:
    dict: Behaviour information

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.save_labels_to_csv
documentation:
Saves the frame labels for each behaviour to a CSV file.

Args:
    video_path (str): Path to the video file
    frame_labels (dict): Labels for each frame
    behaviours (list): List of behaviours

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.ask_behaviours
documentation:
Ask the user for behaviour names via Tkinter dialogs, with optional presets.

Args:
    preset_behaviours (list, optional): List of preset behaviours. Defaults to ['explore_left','explore_right','freezing','grooming'].

Returns:
    list: List of behaviours

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.ask_keys
documentation:
Ask the user for  keys via Tkinter dialogs, with optional presets.

Args:
    behaviours (list): List of behaviours
    preset_keys (list, optional): List of preset keys. Defaults to ['4','6','f','g'].

Returns:
    list: List of keys

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.frame_generator
documentation: Yield frames from the video one by one.

functions.name: rainstorm.labeler.main
documentation: Handle frame labeling for multiple behaviours.
Main function to handle frame labeling for multiple behaviours

modules.name: rainstorm.create_models
documentation: RAINSTORM - @author: Santiago D'hers
Functions for the notebook: 3a-Create_models.ipynb
%% Imports

functions.name: rainstorm.create_models.prepare_data
documentation:
Read the positions and labels into a DataFrame

Args:
    path (str): Path to the colabels file
    labeler_names (list): List of labelers

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Data ready to use
%% Functions

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.choose_example
documentation:
Picks an example file from a list of files.

Args:
    files (list): List of files to choose from.
    filter_word (str, optional): Word to filter files by. Defaults to 'TS'.

Returns:
    str: Name of the chosen file.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the files list is empty.
%% Functions for 1-Prepare_positions.ipynb

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.open_h5_file
documentation:
Opens an h5 file and returns the data as a pandas dataframe.

Args:
    path (str): Path to the h5 file.
    print_data (bool, optional): Whether to print the data. Defaults to False.
    num_sd (float, optional): Number of std_dev away from the mean. Defaults to 2.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: The dataframe contained in.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.filter_and_smooth_df
documentation:
Filters and smooths a DataFrame of coordinates.

Args:
    data (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame of coordinates.
    bodyparts (list): List of bodyparts to filter.
    objects (list): List of objects to filter.
    med_filt_window (int, optional): Window size for median filtering. Defaults to 3.
    drop_below (float, optional): Minimum likelihood to keep a bodypart. Defaults to 0.1.
    num_sd (float, optional): Number of standard deviations to use as the threshold. Defaults to 2.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Filtered and smoothed DataFrame of coordinates.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.find_scale_factor
documentation:
Plots the distance between ears and the mean and median distances.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame containing the coordinates of the points.
    measured_dist (float): Measured distance between points.
    measured_points (list): List of strings containing the names of the points.
    print_results (bool, optional): Whether to print the results. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    scale (float): The scale factor to convert the measured distance to the actual distance.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.process_position_files
documentation:
Processes a list of HDF5 files and saves the smoothed data as a CSV file.

Args:
    files (list): List of HDF5 files to process.
    bodyparts (list): List of objects to process.
    objects (list): List of objects to process.
    measured_dist (float): Measured distance between points in cm.
    measured_points (list): List of reference points for the distance calculation.
    scale (bool): Whether to scale the data.
    fps (int): Frames per second of the video.
    drop_below (float): Drop values if the median likelihood is below this threshold.
    num_sd (float): Number of standard deviations to consider.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.filter_and_move_files
documentation: Filters and moves files to a subfolder.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.plot_position
documentation:
Plot mouse exploration around multiple objects.

Args:
    file (str): Path to the .csv file containing the data.
    objects (list): List of objects to explore.
    maxDistance (float, optional): Maximum distance from the nose to the object. Defaults to 2.5.
    maxAngle (float, optional): Maximum angle between the head-nose and head-object vectors. Defaults to 45.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.plot_freezing
documentation:
Plots freezing events in a video.

Args:
    file (str): Path to the video file.
    fps (int, optional): Frames per second of the video. Defaults to 30.
    threshold (float, optional): Threshold for freezing events. Defaults to 0.01.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.create_movement_and_geolabels
documentation:
Analyzes the position data of a list of files and saves the results to a CSV file.

Args:
    files (list): List of files to analyze.
    objects (list): List of objects to analyze.
    maxDistance (float, optional): Maximum distance between the mouse and the object. Defaults to 2.5.
    maxAngle (float, optional): Maximum angle between the mouse and the object. Defaults to 45.
    fps (int, optional): Frames per second of the video. Defaults to 30.
    freezing_thr (float, optional): Threshold for freezing. Defaults to 0.01.
    darting_thr (float, optional): Threshold for darting. Defaults to 0.8.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.broaden
documentation:
Build the frame window for LSTM training

Args:
    past (int, optional): How many frames into the past. Defaults to 3.
    future (int, optional): How many frames into the future. Defaults to 3.
    broad (float, optional): If you want to extend the reach of your window without increasing the length of the list. Defaults to 1.7.

Returns:
    list: List of frame index that will be used for training

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.prepare_data
documentation:
Read the positions and labels into a DataFrame

Args:
    path (str): Path to the colabels file
    labeler_names (list): List of labelers

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Data ready to use

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.recenter
documentation:
Recenters a DataFrame around a specified point.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to be recentered.
    point (str): Name of the point to be used as the center.
    bodyparts (list): List of bodyparts to be recentered.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Recentered DataFrame.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.reshape
documentation:
Reshapes a DataFrame into a 3D NumPy array.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to reshape.
    past (int, optional): Number of past frames to include. Defaults to 3.
    future (int, optional): Number of future frames to include. Defaults to 3.
    broad (float, optional): Factor to broaden the range of frames. Defaults to 1.7.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: 3D NumPy array.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.create_chimera_and_loo_mean
documentation:
Creates a chimera DataFrame by randomly selecting columns for each row.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to create chimera from.

Returns:
    tuple: A tuple containing the chimera DataFrame and the loo_mean DataFrame.
%% Functions for 3b-Evaluate_models.ipynb

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.smooth_columns
documentation:
Applies smoothing to a DataFrame column.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to apply smoothing to.
    columns (list, optional): List of columns to apply smoothing to. Defaults to [].
    kernel_size (int, optional): Size of the smoothing kernel. Defaults to 3.
    gauss_std (float, optional): Standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel. Defaults to 0.6.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Smoothed DataFrame.

functions.name: rainstorm.rainstorm.compare
documentation:
Compares the predictions of a labeler with the true labels.

Args:
    pred (pd.DataFrame): The predictions of a labeler.
    true (pd.DataFrame): The true labels.
